regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, expect light winds from the north-northeast at 11 knots accompanied by rain showers and a 25% rain probability, with overcast skies currently and at the port. These conditions suggest mostly smooth embarkation and disembarkation operations with limited impact on shore excursions. Looking ahead to the next 24 to 48 hours, Port Everglades and Port Canaveral will experience increasing easterly winds near 14 knots and 36% rain probability, which may affect outdoor activities and vessel maneuvering. Beyond 48 hours, winds will remain steady from the east-northeast at 13 knots with overcast skies and a 31% rain probability in Miami, indicating continued cautious monitoring for potential rain showers around the region.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, winds in Nassau are light from the west at 1 knot under an overcast sky with a 12% rain probability, allowing smooth port operations and minimal impact on shore excursions across the Bahamas and nearby Central Caribbean ports. By day three, easterly winds near 16 knots will continue with a 36% rain probability, which could affect shore excursions in Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Puerto Plata if the drizzle intensifies. Vessel operations should prepare for slightly choppier seas and limited visibility during these periods, particularly in the eastern ports. Passengers and crew should plan accordingly for potential light rain and breezier conditions while underway or ashore.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, Southeast winds at 5 knots and clear skies in San Juan support smooth embarkation and disembarkation with an 18% rain probability posing no operational concerns.
